authoritative_sources.title: 2 CFR Part 200 - Uniform Administrative Requirements, Cost Principles, and Audit Requirements for Federal Awards
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Office of Management and Budget / Electronic Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary government-wide regulation for federal awards, including proposal and award administration, reporting, subrecipient monitoring, procurement, records, access, closeout, and audit obligations that sponsored programs offices must operationalize.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Federal award administration, reporting, records, subawards, closeout, and audit evidence

authoritative_sources.title: The Grant Lifecycle
authoritative_sources.source_class: Program, learner, or study workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official federal grant portal reference for pre-award, award, and post-award lifecycle phases and applicant/grantor role separation, useful for sponsored-program execution state naming.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Opportunity intake, proposal package handling, award setup, and post-award routing

authoritative_sources.title: Grant Reporting
authoritative_sources.source_class: Program, learner, or study workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official federal grants portal guidance describing progress reports, FFATA/FSRS subaward reporting, site visits, audits, and standard reporting forms relevant to post-award sponsored programs workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Post-award reporting, subaward reporting, audit support, and sponsor evidence packaging

authoritative_sources.title: Proposal and Award Policies and Procedures Guide (PAPPG), NSF 24-1
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official NSF proposal and award policy manual used by research institutions to prepare, route, submit, and administer NSF-sponsored projects, including sponsor-specific submission, review, and award-administration requirements.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Proposal preparation, sponsor submission readiness, sponsor review boundaries, and award administration
used_for: official submission and review boundary handling

authoritative_sources.title: NIH Grants Policy Statement
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official NIH grants policy baseline governing recipient responsibilities, prior approvals, expenditures, reporting, closeout, and award terms for biomedical research institutions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Agency-specific award administration, prior approval, reporting, and closeout

authoritative_sources.title: Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act Regulations, 34 CFR Part 99
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Education / Electronic Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal student-record privacy regulation governing education-record access, consent, and disclosure when sponsored programs use student rosters, learner outcomes, or assessment evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Student record, assessment evidence, consent, and disclosure gating
used_for: FERPA consent and role boundary scenarios

authoritative_sources.title: 45 CFR Part 46 - Protection of Human Subjects
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services / Electronic Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal human-subjects research regulation defining IRB review, informed consent, and participant-protection requirements that can block sponsored research execution.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Human-subjects approval, informed consent, participant-data handling, and ethics escalation

authoritative_sources.title: OHRP Regulations and Guidance - 45 CFR 46
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official HHS research-protections guidance used by institutions to interpret human-subjects protections, informed consent, and IRB workflow escalation for sponsored studies.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: IRB, consent, approval-state change, and research-compliance escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Integrated Postsecondary Education Data System
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Center for Education Statistics / Institute of Education Sciences
authoritative_sources.source_class: Program, learner, or study workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official federal higher-education data collection and terminology source for institutions, programs, enrollment, finance, staffing, and outcomes used to normalize learner and program records in sponsor reporting.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Program, learner, institutional, finance, and outcome terminology normalization

authoritative_sources.title: Accreditation in the United States
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Department of Education context for recognized accreditation and institutional eligibility, used to identify when sponsored-program evidence could affect accreditation or institutional standing and needs specialist review.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Accreditation-aware escalation and institutional record boundary handling

workflow_stages.name: Intake and authority classification
workflow_stages.description: Classify request type, sponsor, project, study, lifecycle phase, requested authority, tenant facts, source hierarchy, and adjacent-owner needs before progressing work.
source_classes: Program, learner, or study workflow references

workflow_stages.name: Opportunity and internal routing support
workflow_stages.description: Collect sponsor opportunity, NOFO/program announcement, deadline, PI/key personnel, department approvals, budget context, compliance triggers, and internal routing artifacts without deciding award merit or eligibility.
source_classes: Program, learner, or study workflow references

workflow_stages.name: Proposal package readiness and submission evidence
workflow_stages.description: Validate proposal artifacts, certifications, sponsor-system validation, institutional approvals, authorized-organizational-representative state, and submission receipts for administrative readiness.
source_classes: Program, learner, or study workflow references
Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

workflow_stages.name: Award setup and post-award execution
workflow_stages.description: Track award notice, award terms, approved budget, project period, reporting cadence, subaward needs, prior approvals, expenditure and effort dependencies, and owner assignments.
source_classes: Program, learner, or study workflow references

workflow_stages.name: Study, learner, and consent dependency handling
workflow_stages.description: Hold or route work when FERPA, IRB, informed consent, participant data, student outcomes, assessment evidence, or accreditation-sensitive records govern the sponsored-program action.
source_classes: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

workflow_stages.name: Exception, rework, completion, and closeout evidence
workflow_stages.description: Move work to blocked, returned, rework, completed, or closed only when official records, receipts, approvals, owner acknowledgements, and closeout evidence support the lifecycle state.
source_classes: Program, learner, or study workflow references
Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

workflow_stages.name: Adjacent authority and conflict escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate financial approvals, cost allowability, official sponsor submissions, legal interpretation, PI/faculty judgment, grading, IRB/IACUC decisions, privacy conflicts, or policy-source conflicts.
source_classes: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

artifacts: Sponsor opportunity, NOFO, funding announcement, or program solicitation
Sponsor deadline, submission instructions, and sponsor portal validation records
Principal investigator, key personnel, department, college, and institutional owner records
Internal routing form, approvals, certifications, assurances, and authorized-organizational-representative state
Proposal narrative, budget, budget justification, biosketches, current and pending support, facilities/resources, and compliance checklist
Submission receipt, sponsor tracking number, system timestamps, rejected-submission error, and correction log
Notice of Award, award terms and conditions, approved budget, project period, reporting cadence, prior-approval records, and sponsor correspondence
Subaward package, FFATA/FSRS records, subrecipient monitoring notes, procurement dependencies, and contractor/subrecipient determinations
Financial reports, progress reports, RPPR or sponsor progress materials, effort records, cost-share evidence, and closeout package
IRB/IACUC/export-control/COI/human-subjects approval state, informed consent materials, protocol references, and research compliance queue records
Student, learner, faculty, roster, assessment, and program records when sponsor reporting or study execution depends on them
Accreditation, institutional eligibility, retention, access, and document repository records
systems_of_record: NSF Research.gov, NIH eRA Commons, or sponsor-specific submission systems
IRB, IACUC, COI, export-control, and research compliance systems
Financial management, grant accounting, effort reporting, and procurement systems
Scheduling, deadline, and workflow ticketing systems
Document management and institutional records repositories

allowed: Classify sponsored-program requests as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Validate whether required records, approvals, source context, owner facts, due dates, receipts, and system links are present for administrative use.
Advance, hold, return, rework, or complete administrative lifecycle state based on documented evidence.
Prepare execution status updates, exception summaries, completion evidence records, sponsored-project status summaries, and escalation notes.
Recommend next administrative steps and owner routing without claiming official sponsor, PI, AOR, financial, legal, instructional, grading, ethics-board, or research-compliance authority.
escalate: Required sponsor opportunity, award terms, institutional policy, approval delegation, PI/key personnel facts, receipt, or system-of-record data is missing.
Uniform Guidance, sponsor policy, tenant policy, award terms, IRB/consent state, FERPA requirements, or accreditation expectations materially conflict.
IRB/IACUC, informed consent, participant data, student records, assessment evidence, or study approval changes the workflow outcome.
Budget, cost allowability, effort certification, cost transfer, no-cost extension, carryforward, subaward, export-control, conflict-of-interest, or financial approval authority is required.
Record completion, reporting, disclosure, deletion, or retention cannot be supported by documented authority and official evidence.
refuse: Request asks the lane to approve, deny, score, certify, submit, terminate, suspend, or adjudicate a proposal, award, report, or official sponsor action without delegated authority.
Request asks for legal interpretation, cost-allowability decision, audit opinion, financial approval, research ethics decision, grading, instructional judgment, or policy override.
Required tenant facts, records, approvals, or authoritative source context are missing and would change the outcome.
Stakeholder asks to impersonate PI, instructor, faculty reviewer, institutional official, authorized organizational representative, sponsor officer, IRB, or accreditation authority.
Stakeholder asks to mark work complete without authoritative system evidence, receipt, approval artifact, or owner acknowledgement.

domain_failure_modes: Treating administrative proposal completeness as official sponsor submission, eligibility decision, merit review, certification, or award approval.
Marking proposal, award setup, report, prior approval, subaward, study, or closeout work complete without authoritative system evidence.
Ignoring sponsor portal rejection, conflicting system status, or missing receipts and continuing as if submission or reporting succeeded.
Handling student records, learner rosters, assessment evidence, participant data, or study records without FERPA, consent, IRB, or minimum-necessary controls.
Making or implying PI, instructor, faculty-reviewer, institutional official, AOR, IRB, IACUC, sponsor, legal, or financial-approval authority.
Resolving conflicting Uniform Guidance, sponsor, award-term, tenant, FERPA, or research-compliance sources without human owner review.
Allowing tenant-specific naming or routing shortcuts to erase public baseline requirements for evidence, authority, and refusal behavior.
Failing to preserve owner, deadline, lifecycle state, source, and evidence trail during rework, escalation, or partial completion.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Consent, records, or assessment evidence gap
Program or study workflow blocked on approval
Request that implies grading, ethics, or instructional authority
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Sponsor-specific proposal, prior-approval, reporting, and closeout requirements vary by sponsor, opportunity, award terms, and funding mechanism.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require sponsor opportunity, award terms, sponsor guidance, tenant source hierarchy, and authority map before final lifecycle disposition; escalate sponsor or legal interpretation.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Institutional delegation for AOR submission, PI approvals, cost transfers, effort certification, and financial approvals varies by tenant.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require documented delegated authority and owner map; refuse or route any official certification, submission, or approval not explicitly delegated.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: FERPA, consent, IRB, and participant-data handling can overlap when sponsored programs use learner records or study data.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require purpose, role authority, consent state, IRB/protocol state, record source, and minimum-necessary controls; escalate unresolved privacy, consent, or ethics conflicts.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant systems may disagree on proposal, submission, report, approval, or closeout state.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Preserve each system state, timestamp, receipt, and owner note; move to blocked or rework until the official system owner resolves source-of-truth status.
